machinery_genes.quote: We report that InvG, PrgH, and PrgK constitute the base and that PrgI is the main component of the needle of the type III secretion complex.
machinery_genes.note: Strain S. Typhimurium SPI-1. Stoichiometry 15-mer (16-mer in periplasmic coupling domain) confirmed in Hu et al. 2019 (10.1038/s41564-019-0545-z).

machinery_genes.quote: We report that InvG, PrgH, and PrgK constitute the base and that PrgI is the main component of the needle of the type III secretion complex.
We report that InvG, PrgH, and PrgK constitute the base and that PrgI is the main component of the needle of the type III secretion complex.
We report that InvG, PrgH, and PrgK constitute the base and that PrgI is the main component of the needle of the type III secretion complex.
machinery_genes.note: S. Typhimurium SPI-1.

machinery_genes.role: inner rod (couples needle to base; initiates/anchors needle polymerization)
machinery_genes.quote: the PrgJ rod directly binds and functions as an initiator and anchor of needle polymerization.
machinery_genes.note: Also identified as a needle-complex component in Kimbrough & Miller 2000 (10.1073/pnas.200209497) and stoichiometry ~6 PrgJ in Zilkenat et al. 2016 (10.1074/mcp.M115.056598).

machinery_genes.quote: we provide evidence that the export apparatus of type III secretion systems contains five SpaP, one SpaQ, one SpaR, and one SpaS.
machinery_genes.note: S. Typhimurium SPI-1. SpaPQR localized within the IM rings in Hu et al. 2019 (10.1038/s41564-019-0545-z) as the 'SpaPQR export apparatus'.

machinery_genes.quote: we provide evidence that the export apparatus of type III secretion systems contains five SpaP, one SpaQ, one SpaR, and one SpaS.
machinery_genes.note: S. Typhimurium SPI-1.

machinery_genes.quote: we provide evidence that the export apparatus of type III secretion systems contains five SpaP, one SpaQ, one SpaR, and one SpaS.
machinery_genes.note: S. Typhimurium SPI-1.

machinery_genes.quote: we provide evidence that the export apparatus of type III secretion systems contains five SpaP, one SpaQ, one SpaR, and one SpaS.
machinery_genes.note: S. Typhimurium SPI-1. SctU autoprotease/switch by homology; quote names it as an export-apparatus component.

machinery_genes.quote: We confirmed that the previously suggested stoichiometry of nine InvA is valid for assembled needle complexes and describe a loose association of InvA with other needle complex components that may reflect its function.
machinery_genes.note: S. Typhimurium SPI-1. InvA (SctV) is the membrane export apparatus required for needle assembly (Kubori et al. 2000, 10.1073/pnas.170128997).

machinery_genes.quote: We detected several T3SS-related proteins including components of the needle complex and export apparatus, the cytoplasmic proteins OrgA, OrgB and InvC, as well as the protein translocases SipB, SipC and SipD.
machinery_genes.note: S. Typhimurium SPI-1. Explicitly called 'the ATPase InvC' later in the same paper.

machinery_genes.quote: the cytoplasmic proteins SpaO, OrgA, and OrgB form part of a large molecular weight multiprotein complex that can include components of the needle complex and export apparatus.
machinery_genes.note: S. Typhimurium SPI-1. SpaO/OrgA/OrgB constitute the sorting platform.

machinery_genes.quote: the cytoplasmic proteins SpaO, OrgA, and OrgB form part of a large molecular weight multiprotein complex that can include components of the needle complex and export apparatus.
machinery_genes.note: S. Typhimurium SPI-1.

machinery_genes.quote: the cytoplasmic proteins SpaO, OrgA, and OrgB form part of a large molecular weight multiprotein complex that can include components of the needle complex and export apparatus.
machinery_genes.note: S. Typhimurium SPI-1.

machinery_genes.quote: We report here that Salmonella protein InvE, which is also encoded within SPI-1, is essential for the translocation of bacterial proteins into host cells. An S. enterica serovar Typhimurium mutant carrying a loss-of-function mutation in invE shows reduced secretion of SipB, SipC, and SipD while exhibiting increased secretion of other TTSS effector proteins.
machinery_genes.note: S. Typhimurium SPI-1. SctW/YopN-family gatekeeper; controls deployment of translocators vs effectors. Not a chaperone or effector.

machinery_genes.quote: We show that the length of the needle segment is determined by the type III secretion associated protein InvJ.
machinery_genes.note: S. Typhimurium SPI-1.

machinery_genes.quote: InvH is an outer membrane lipoprotein that is processed by signal peptidase II. ... InvH was shown to be required for the proper localization of InvG in the outer membrane and for the secretion of the virulence factor SipC.
machinery_genes.note: S. Typhimurium SPI-1. Pilotin for the InvG secretin.

machinery_genes.quote: We detected several T3SS-related proteins including components of the needle complex and export apparatus, the cytoplasmic proteins OrgA, OrgB and InvC, as well as the protein translocases SipB, SipC and SipD.
machinery_genes.note: S. Typhimurium SPI-1. Apparatus (host-membrane pore), per brief, despite being secreted. SipB/SipC/SipD form the needle tip-translocon (Glasgow et al. 2017, 10.1021/acssynbio.6b00335).

machinery_genes.quote: We detected several T3SS-related proteins including components of the needle complex and export apparatus, the cytoplasmic proteins OrgA, OrgB and InvC, as well as the protein translocases SipB, SipC and SipD.
machinery_genes.note: S. Typhimurium SPI-1.

machinery_genes.quote: We detected several T3SS-related proteins including components of the needle complex and export apparatus, the cytoplasmic proteins OrgA, OrgB and InvC, as well as the protein translocases SipB, SipC and SipD.
machinery_genes.note: S. Typhimurium SPI-1. SipD is the needle-tip complex protein (Glasgow et al. 2017, 10.1021/acssynbio.6b00335: 'The needle tip complex in Salmonella enterica consists of three translocon proteins: SipB, SipC, and SipD').

notes: SPI-1 (Inv/Spa/Prg/Org) injectisome of S. Typhimurium, disambiguated from the flagellar T3SS (Flh/Fli/Flg) and from SPI-2 (Ssa). Core injectisome apparatus fully covered: secretin InvG; basal-body rings PrgH (SctD) and PrgK (SctJ); export apparatus SpaP/SpaQ/SpaR/SpaS (SctR/S/T/U) and InvA (SctV); ATPase InvC (SctN); sorting platform SpaO/OrgA/OrgB (SctQ/K/L); inner rod PrgJ (SctI); needle PrgI (SctF); length ruler InvJ (SctP); gatekeeper InvE (SctW); pilotin InvH; translocon SipB/SipC/SipD. Most components characterized in S. Typhimurium SPI-1 (strain SL1344/14028/ATCC14028-derived lines) which is the same SPI-1 locus present in LT2 (NC_003197.2). Per curation rules, EXCLUDED as non-machinery: effectors (SipA, SptP, SopE, etc.), translocase/effector chaperones (SicA, SicP, InvB), and regulators (HilA, HilD, InvF). InvI (SctO, ATPase stalk) is part of this system by homology and operon membership but was NOT listed because no verbatim literature quote naming it as an SPI-1 apparatus component was located within scope; this is the one core-stalk gap. InvE is listed as gatekeeper (SctW) on the Kubori & Galan 2002 quote; note Lara-Tejero 2011 also describes InvE acting as a chaperone for the Sip translocases, but its SctW gatekeeper identity is the machinery role recorded here.
